item.title: BCE's Rehn: navigating geopolitical turmoil
item.description: Finland's central bank governor Olli Rehn discussed the challenge of navigating turbulent geopolitics for central banks.

item.title: Chinese government bonds: a safe haven in times of war
item.description: Yields on Chinese government bonds have fallen marginally since the start of the conflict, while those of other major economies have risen.

item.title: The ECB’s three-pronged monetary strategy
item.description: Unless the war ends quickly, a rise in Eurozone interest rates looks inevitable

item.title: March Eurozone inflation reflects Iran war impact
item.description: Underlying price pressures were muted, reducing concerns about high energy inflation

item.title: Eurozone inflation jumps to 2.5% in March
item.description: Middle East energy shock pushes annual rate past European Central Bank’s 2% target

item.title: European fiscal support for high energy prices still targeted and temporary
item.description: Central banks in the region have difficult trade-offs to make and government action generally complicates things

item.title: The Cost of Hesitation: Why “Finishing the Mission” is Imperative in Iran
item.description: Editor’s Note: War on the Rocks is running “for” and “against” articles on the war with Iran by legislators with military backgrounds. You can find the other article here.The view from a C-130 during a combat mission in the Middle East provides clarity that is often lost in the sanitized briefing rooms of Washington. As an Air Force medical crew director, I have looked into the eyes of young servicemembers being medevacked out of theater, and I lived through what decades of strategic inconsistency asks of our warfighters. As a board-certified psychiatric mental health nurse practitioner, I am equally attuned The post The Cost of Hesitation: Why “Finishing the Mission” is Imperative in Iran appeared first on War on the Rocks .

item.title: Holding Water Hostage
item.description: Parts of the Middle East — especially around the Persian Gulf — rely on desalinated water. Worst-case scenarios for war in the region have often included attacks on desalination facilities, and the war with Iran that began on Feb. 28 has raised the potential for such a scenario. So far, there have been reports that a U.S. strike on Qeshm Island damaged an Iranian desalination plant and that an Iranian drone had hit a desalination plant in Bahrain. On March 30, an attack damaged a building at a power and desalination site in Kuwait. Iran has warned that it could The post Holding Water Hostage appeared first on War on the Rocks .

item.title: Pakistan brings China into Iran mediation, unveils 5-point plan
item.description: Pakistan brings China into Iran mediation, unveils 5-point plan

item.title: Japan's struggling automakers keep an eye on Trump Fed pick Warsh
item.description: Japan's struggling automakers keep an eye on Trump Fed pick Warsh

item.title: Indonesia pushes remote work, biodiesel expansion to tackle oil crisis
item.description: Indonesia pushes remote work, biodiesel expansion to tackle oil crisis

item.title: The Trump Administration Had No Plan for the War with Iran
item.description: Editor’s Note: War on the Rocks is running “for” and “against” articles on the war with Iran by legislators with military backgrounds. You can find the other article here.21 years ago, I was a Marine infantryman in Anbar province, western Iraq, carrying so much gear that I could barely feel my legs. My job was to go town to town, search houses, and try to stop insurgents before they stopped us. Every day, someone was trying to kill us — improvised explosive devices buried in the roads, mortars in the middle of the night, and random rocket propelled grenades fired The post The Trump Administration Had No Plan for the War with Iran appeared first on War on the Rocks .

item.title: OPEC output plunges due to war
item.description: OPEC's oil production dropped by 7.2 million barrels per day due to the war.

item.title: $150 Oil Could Tip World Economy Into Recession
item.description: The world economy is heading for a “rare” recession in the middle of this year as a prolonged war seems likely amid the prospect of US troops heading to the Middle East. Economists have warned that activity will fall in the middle of the year if oil prices surge to $150 per barrel and remain there for a period of four months. Oil prices continued to climb higher on Monday as the Brent Crude benchmark raced past the $116 per barrel mark amid mixed messages between the US, Israel and Iran on the state of the war. It…

item.title: India Boosts Diesel Exports to Southeast Asia to 7-Year High
item.description: Tightening Asian fuel markets and Indian refiners’ thirst for Russian crude have resulted in the highest Indian diesel exports to Southeast Asia in seven and a half years in March, Reuters reported on Tuesday, citing trade sources and data from analytics firm Kpler. As the Middle East war roils Asian fuel markets and prompted many countries in Asia, including China, to impose fuel export bans, Indian refiners are taking advantage of the higher premiums they get to send their fuel to Southeast Asia, which began struggling with fuel shortages.…

item.title: E-commerce giant Mercado Libre to sunset rewards-based Mercado Coin: Report
item.description: The closure of Mercado Coin doesn't mark the end of Mercado Libre's crypto rewards strategy as it still offers the Meli Dolar (MUSD), a US-dollar stablecoin which launched in 2024.

item.title: U.S. Senators Unveil ‘Mined in America Act’ to Reshore BTC Mining, Codify Bitcoin Strategic Reserve
item.description: Bitcoin Magazine U.S. Senators Unveil ‘Mined in America Act’ to Reshore BTC Mining, Codify Bitcoin Strategic Reserve Senators Bill Cassidy and Cynthia Lummis introduced the “Mined in America Act” to reshore U.S. bitcoin mining, reduce reliance on Chinese hardware, and codify a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve into law. This post U.S. Senators Unveil ‘Mined in America Act’ to Reshore BTC Mining, Codify Bitcoin Strategic Reserve first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

item.title: India’s Aviation Market Rocked by Wild Jet Fuel Price Swings
item.description: Indian Oil Corp. earlier today raised jet fuel prices for local airlines significantly, before cutting them sharply hours later, media are reporting, as the world’s third-largest importer of crude grapples with the fallout of the Middle East war. Jet fuel prices in India surged twofold earlier in the day, the Indian Express reported, hitting an all-time high of 207,341 rupees, equal to almost $2,200 per kilolitre in New Delhi, as the country’s largest oil company sought to manage the impact of oil import disruptions. Hours later, however,…

item.title: Guyana's Oil Boom Will Boost Energy Security in the Americas
item.description: Guyana, once among South America's most impoverished nations, is experiencing an epic oil boom. In a mere four years, the country of less than one million went from first discovery to first oil and is now lifting over 900,000 barrels per day. There are signs of significant production growth ahead. This is occurring at a crucial time for a world experiencing an energy crisis after oil spiked to well-over $110 per barrel after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation for U.S. and Israeli airstrikes. Guyana's oil boom is significantly reducing…

item.title: Bitcoin, stocks soar as markets respond to chance of US and Israel-Iran war ending
item.description: Bitcoin held gains above $68,000 as investors leaned into news that the US and Iran were ideating ways to end the war. Will markets hold their newfound bullishness?

item.title: Donald Trump says US will leave Iran within 2 to 3 weeks
item.description: The conflict in Iran has been escalating following US and Israeli strikes against Iran in February, which sent oil prices higher and crypto and broader markets tumbling.

item.title: Bitcoin hits $68K but BTC futures, macro data show traders remain bearish
item.description: Bitcoin rallied to $68,000 as markets responded positively to the prospect of the US and Israel-Iran war ending, but data shows futures traders are not convinced.
